assertions.title: Command Execution - Shell commands execute correctly inside containers with proper exit codes and output capture

steps.expected: Command 'echo hello world' should return exit code 0, stdout contains 'hello world', stderr is empty
steps.observed: Test passed - exit_code: 0, stdout contained 'hello world', stderr was empty

steps.expected: Command 'exit 42' should return exit code 42; nonexistent command should return non-zero exit code with error in stderr
steps.observed: Test passed - exit code 42 for explicit failure, non-zero exit code with stderr error message for nonexistent command

steps.expected: Command 'sleep 5' with 1 second timeout should return exit code -1 and stderr indicating timeout
steps.observed: Test passed - exit_code: -1, stderr contained 'timed out'

testOutput: test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

assertions.title: File Operations - Files can be written to and read from containers correctly

steps.expected: Write file 'test_file.txt' with content 'Hello, World!', read back and verify content matches
steps.observed: Test passed - write succeeded, read returned exact content 'Hello, World!'

steps.expected: Create subdirectory and write file 'subdir/nested.txt' with content, verify directory and file exist
steps.observed: Test passed - subdirectory created, file written and readable with matching content

steps.expected: Attempt to read 'nonexistent_file_xyz.txt' should return error
steps.observed: Test passed - read returned Err as expected

steps.expected: Write multiple files (file1.txt, file2.txt, dir/file3.txt), read each back and verify with find command
steps.observed: Test passed - all files written and verified, content matched, find command located all .txt files

testOutput: test result: ok. 4 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

summary: Tested 2 assertions with 7 total test cases: VAL-DOCKER-003 (3 tests - all passed) confirms correct command execution with exit codes and output capture; VAL-DOCKER-004 (4 tests - all passed) confirms file write/read operations work correctly including subdirectory creation and error handling for non-existent files.
